diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..a9910bf
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+/**
+ * NotificationAdapter interface — the contract all notification delivery adapters must implement.
+ *
+ * Architecture constraint: Every notification channel (email, webhook, in-app, SMS, etc.)
+ * must be implemented as a NotificationAdapter. The NotificationService dispatches to
+ * adapters based on the notification's channel field.
+ *
+ * To add a new notification channel:
+ * 1. Add the channel name to NotificationChannel in src/models/notification.ts
+ * 2. Create an adapter class implementing this interface in src/adapters/
+ * 3. Register the adapter with NotificationService via registerAdapter()
+ */
+
+import type { NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult } from '../models/notification.js';
+
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /** Which channel this adapter handles */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification. Adapters must handle their own error cases
+   * and return a NotificationResult indicating success or failure.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}

diff --git a/src/models/notification.ts b/src/models/notification.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..75d507a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/models/notification.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,63 @@
+/**
+ * Notification model — core types for the notification system.
+ *
+ * Architecture: Event-driven notifications via EventBus.
+ * All notifications flow through: EventBus -> NotificationService -> NotificationAdapter(s).
+ *
+ * Constraints:
+ * - All notification delivery must go through a NotificationAdapter implementation.
+ * - All notification triggers must originate from EventBus events.
+ * - NotificationService is the sole subscriber that translates events into notifications.
+ */
+
+/**
+ * Supported notification channels. Each channel has a corresponding adapter.
+ */
+export type NotificationChannel = 'email' | 'webhook' | 'in-app';
+
+/**
+ * Notification types correspond to the kinds of events that trigger notifications.
+ */
+export type NotificationType =
+  | 'order-confirmation'
+  | 'order-status-update'
+  | 'welcome'
+  | 'user-updated';
+
+/**
+ * The payload passed to notification adapters for delivery.
+ */
+export interface NotificationPayload {
+  /** Unique notification ID */
+  id: string;
+  /** What kind of notification this is */
+  type: NotificationType;
+  /** Which channel to deliver on */
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  /** Recipient identifier (email address, user ID, webhook URL, etc.) */
+  recipient: string;
+  /** Notification subject / title */
+  subject: string;
+  /** Notification body content */
+  body: string;
+  /** When the notification was created */
+  createdAt: Date;
+  /** Optional structured metadata for adapter-specific use */
+  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Result returned by adapters after attempting delivery.
+ */
+export interface NotificationResult {
+  /** Whether delivery succeeded */
+  success: boolean;
+  /** The notification that was sent */
+  notificationId: string;
+  /** Channel that handled delivery */
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  /** Timestamp of delivery attempt */
+  deliveredAt: Date;
+  /** Error message if delivery failed */
+  error?: string;
+}

prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-wLO3sb.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: architecture choice (event-driven vs direct calls), core types, notification service, email adapter

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * All order lifecycle events (creation, status changes) are emitted via EventBus.
 * The NotificationService subscribes to these events independently.
 */
parameters.old_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture note: Order events are used to trigger side effects
 * (notifications, analytics, etc.) without direct coupling.
 * The OrderService emits events; it does not directly call
 * the NotificationService.
 */

cons: Harder to add new notification channels
alternatives.reason_rejected: The codebase already invested in EventBus with typed events; CallbackRegistry was the exception, not the rule. Direct calls would mean more coupling and harder extensibility for 11 other developers building on this.

cons: Two patterns to learn and maintain
Codebase comments explicitly flag this as problematic
alternatives.option: Mixed pattern (keep both EventBus and CallbackRegistry)
alternatives.reason_rejected: The existing code explicitly documented this as an anti-pattern that should be unified.

assumptions: EventBus remains the single event infrastructure (no migration to external message broker)
Notifications are fire-and-forget from the emitter's perspective
Adapter implementations handle their own error recovery
constraints: All notification triggers must originate from EventBus events — never call NotificationService.send() directly from business services
All notification delivery must go through a NotificationAdapter implementation registered via registerAdapter()
To add a new channel: implement NotificationAdapter interface, register with NotificationService
To add a new notification trigger: add event type to AppEvent union, subscribe in NotificationService.registerEventHandlers()
One adapter per channel — registering a second adapter for the same channel replaces the first
parameters.context: The codebase had an inconsistent mix of EventBus (for order:created) and CallbackRegistry (for order:status-changed) patterns for triggering notifications. Task required choosing ONE pattern and building a clean foundation for 11 other developers.
parameters.rationale: EventBus was already the primary pattern in the codebase with typed events, event logging, and async support. The codebase comments explicitly flagged the mixed pattern as tech debt. EventBus provides: (1) loose coupling between services and notification delivery, (2) typed event payloads via AppEvent union, (3) event log for debugging/testing, (4) easy addition of new event subscribers without modifying emitters. The adapter pattern on the delivery side enables adding new channels (webhook, SMS, in-app) without modifying NotificationService.
parameters.summary: Notification system uses pure event-driven architecture via EventBus with pluggable NotificationAdapter pattern

parameters.detail:
Implemented notification system foundation:

1. **Architecture**: Pure event-driven via EventBus (unified — removed CallbackRegistry usage from OrderService).
2. **Core types** (`src/models/notification.ts`): NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult.
3. **Adapter interface** (`src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts`): NotificationAdapter contract — all delivery channels must implement this.
4. **Email adapter** (`src/adapters/email.adapter.ts`): EmailAdapter implementing NotificationAdapter with in-memory stub.
5. **NotificationService rewrite** (`src/services/notification.service.ts`): Subscribes to EventBus events, dispatches to registered adapters. Supports registerAdapter() for plugging in channels.
6. **OrderService fix** (`src/services/order.service.ts`): Status changes now emit via EventBus instead of CallbackRegistry.
7. **Tests updated**: All 70 tests pass, clean TypeScript compile.

Key constraints for other agents:
- All notifications triggered ONLY via EventBus.emit()
- All delivery via NotificationAdapter implementations registered with registerAdapter()
- New channels: implement NotificationAdapter + register it
- New triggers: add event to AppEvent union + subscribe in registerEventHandlers()
parameters.summary: Agent 1: Notification system foundation complete — pure EventBus architecture, adapter pattern, email adapter, all 70 tests pass

parameters.detail: The CallbackRegistry in src/utils/callback-registry.ts and the statusChangeCallbacks singleton still exist but are no longer used by OrderService or NotificationService. All notification triggers now go through EventBus. Do not use CallbackRegistry for new notification triggers — this was the inconsistency that was explicitly cleaned up.
parameters.summary: CallbackRegistry is now unused for notifications — do not reintroduce it for notification triggers

parameters.command: git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Implement event-driven notification system foundation

Unify notification triggers on EventBus (removing CallbackRegistry usage),
introduce NotificationAdapter interface for pluggable delivery channels,
and add EmailAdapter stub. This provides a clean foundation for 11 other
developers to build notification features against.

Key architectural constraints:
- All notifications triggered via EventBus.emit() only
- All delivery through NotificationAdapter implementations
- New channels: implement NotificationAdapter + registerAdapter()
- New triggers: add to AppEvent union + subscribe in registerEventHandlers()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
